400.a: To send players into the air for fast movement and gliding

400.a: A crown won by recent winners that can be carried into the next match

400.q: What must you pick up to reboot an eliminated teammate?
What happens if a teammate's Reboot Card expires?
400.a: They can no longer be rebooted

400.q: What does third partying mean?
400.a: Attacking players who are already fighting each other

400.q: What does rotating mean in Fortnite?
400.a: Moving and repositioning toward the next safe zone or stronger position

400.q: Which building material has the highest durability?
What does Overshield do in Zero Build?
400.a: It gives players extra regenerating protection

400.q: What is the Battle Pass?
400.a: A seasonal progression track filled with unlockable rewards

400.q: What are NPCs in Fortnite?
400.a: Non-player characters that can sell items, give quests, or interact with players

400.q: What does UEFN stand for?
What does editing a build piece allow you to do?
400.a: Change its shape or openings for movement, defense, or shots

400.q: What advantage does high ground usually give?
400.a: Better angles and vertical control in fights

400.q: What is siphon in Fortnite?
400.a: Health or shield gained after getting an elimination in certain modes or rulesets

400.q: What does boxed up mean?
400.a: A player is enclosed inside structures for protection

400.q: What is a hot drop?
400.a: Landing immediately at a crowded, contested location

What is the OG Season that brought back the old map?
600.a: Chapter 4 Season OG

600.a: Drum Gun

600.q: What competitive format does Fortnite use for tournaments?
600.a: Arena / FNCS

600.q: What is the highest arena rank?
600.a: Champion

600.q: What crossover introduced lightsabers to Fortnite?
600.a: Star Wars

600.q: What is the Zero Point in Fortnite lore?
600.a: A powerful reality-warping energy nexus tied to the island and the multiverse

600.q: Who is Agent Jones?
600.a: An Imagined Order agent, often called Jones, who became central to the island's reality storyline

600.q: What organization secretly controlled the island for years?
600.a: The Imagined Order

600.q: Who are The Seven?
600.a: A group opposing the Imagined Order and trying to break the Loop

600.q: What is the Loop in Fortnite lore?
600.a: A repeating, memory-wiping cycle that traps contestants on the island

600.q: What event connected Chapter 1 and Chapter 2?
600.a: The Black Hole event (The End)

600.q: What does piece control mean?
600.a: Claiming the builds around an opponent to limit their movement and options

600.q: What is tarping in competitive Fortnite?
600.a: Building protective tunnels to rotate safely through crowded zones

600.q: What is box fighting?
600.a: Close-range fighting centered around small structures, edits, and tight peeks

600.q: What is Storm Surge?
600.a: A competitive mechanic that damages players if lobbies are too stacked and teams have dealt too little damage

600.q: What is a right-hand peek?
600.a: Using an angle that exposes less of your body because of camera positioning

600.q: What does deadside mean during a rotation?
600.a: The quieter side of zone with fewer opponents nearby

600.q: What is a full box?
600.a: Completely surrounding a player with your own structures

600.q: What does taking height mean?
600.a: Winning vertical control over an opponent in a fight

600.q: Why are edit resets important?
600.a: They let you close your build back up after opening it for a shot or peek

600.q: What is a refresh in late-game Fortnite?
600.a: An elimination or pickup that gives you extra mats, heals, or loot to keep surviving

600.q: What does phasing through builds mean?
600.a: Moving through structures due to momentum, timing, or collision quirks

600.q: What does tunneling mean in endgame?
600.a: Chaining protective builds together while moving through zone

600.q: Why is mats management so important in build modes?
600.a: Running out of materials removes your main defense and mobility tool

600.q: What is a surge tag?
600.a: Damage dealt mainly to help stay above Storm Surge thresholds
